selected_important_turn.text: We'll be able to make it close to equal. Let's do 2 foods for one of us, 2 waters and 2 firewood for the other person. I'll take either.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation proposal from YOU and it frames the negotiation toward a fair, equal split — it shapes expectations and anchors the deal space.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is vague about who gets which bundle and treats items as identical instead of tying allocations to revealed preferences (both parties value food most). Saying “I'll take either” reduces use of preference information to maximize joint value and misses an integrative opportunity to explicitly assign items to match priorities.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Make the offer more preference-sensitive and justificatory: propose a specific allocation that gives the other person the item they prioritized (food) while explaining the tradeoff (e.g., you take 2 food because you said food is top, I take 2 water because I hike); be explicit about why this split is fair and how it uses both parties' stated priorities.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: moderate increase (better use of preference info creates a higher-value, more stable split)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (explicit rationale shows respect for the other's top priority)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces back-and-forth by proposing a clear, preference-aligned allocation)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (shows listening and fairness rather than indifference)

value2reason.Low: Firewood can be located around camp
value2reason.Medium: Need to stay hyrdrated from all the hiking
value2reason.High: Need many snacks throughout the day for energy to hike

value2reason.Medium: We plan to do a lot of difficult hikes.
value2reason.High: I have a teenage son who eats a lot!

dialogue.text: Hello fellow camper! I am interested to know your preference in items?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hello! Yes I'm excited for the trip. 🙂 My most important is food. How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am excited too. I really need food too. What other item is priority for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I also need water because we plan to do a lot of hiking. What about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Wow, I think we planned the same activities for our trip. I need water too 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Haha well we'll be seeing a lot of each other then. Is water your number one or two priority?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Water is my second priority item. I am sure we can work out a fair deal. What do you propose?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We'll be able to make it close to equal. Let's do 2 foods for one of us, 2 waters and 2 firewood for the other person. I'll take either.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that’s a good start. Let me propose this: I’ll take 2 Food and 2 Water, and you can have the remaining 1 Food, 1 Water, and all 3 Firewood. That way, we both get what we need, and I can focus on energy for hiking.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that’s a fair offer. Let me adjust slightly to match our priorities. I’ll take 2 Food and 1 Water, and you can have 1 Food, 2 Water, and all 3 Firewood. This way, I get more of what I value most, and you get what you need for hiking. How does that sound?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your reasoning. Let me accept this deal. I’ll take 1 Food, 2 Water, and all 3 Firewood. You’ll have 2 Food and 1 Water. That works for me. Let’s head out!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
